_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize AK state legislature batch: frier (House 40), tilton (Senate M), olson (Senate T), gray-jackson (Senate G), dunbar (Senate J). Evidence-first; null where no public record; cross-party deviations credited (Olson pro-life/tough-on-crime D; Dunbar pro-police D). Candidacy currency: Olson & Dunbar are NOT on 2026 ballot (terms to Jan 2029).

profile.background: Iñupiaq; first-term AK House District 40 (Utqiagvik/North Slope), assumed office Jan 2025. Former North Slope Borough school board president. Co-Chair House Resources; member of bipartisan House Majority Coalition. Legally changed name from Robyn Niayuq Burke to Frier on 2025-10-15.

robyn-niayuq-frier.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. First-term member with a thin, narrow record (rural education/resource/subsistence). Did not respond to Alaska Family Action 2024 Values Voter Guide; no NRA / AK Right to Life / AFC rating exists. Confirmed seeking reelection 2026 vs Saima Chase (D). Signed U.S. Term Limits Article V pledge (Jan 2026) — notable but not a rubric cell. Pro-public-school-funding posture (BSA increase, HB174, veto-override caucus) cuts against school-choice but is not a clean contrary cell. No culture-war votes/positions found in any of the 49 cells -> all null (honest, not penalized). Name change Burke->Frier 2025-10-15.

profile.background: Appointed to AK Senate District M (Mat-Su/Wasilla) by Gov. Dunleavy on 2025-11-26 (assumed 2025-11-29), succeeding Shelley Hughes. Previously AK House (2015-2025); Speaker of the House 2023-2025. Appointed incumbent running for the District M seat in 2026 (primary Aug 18, general Nov 3). ACU/CPAC conservative scorecard in the 90s.

cathy-tilton.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Office corrected/confirmed: now State Senator District M (appointed Nov 2025), formerly House Speaker. Strong documented conservative record. SANCTITY: voted FOR the 2023 amendment to bar Medicaid funding of elective abortions (one of the majority; not among the 5 GOP defectors); voted NO on HB195/2026 (the bill AK pro-life groups opposed as chemical-abortion expansion) -> pro-life side. MARRIAGE: as Speaker scheduled and her GOP majority passed HB183 (trans-girls-in-sports ban, 22-18, May 2024). ECON: introduced HJR7 constitutional spending cap; Finance budget cuts. ELECTIONS: opposed eliminating absentee-ballot witness-signature requirement (favored stricter absentee integrity). SELF-DEFENSE: championed HB61 (signed 2023), barring officials from shutting gun stores / restricting firearms in declared emergencies (NRA-backed). No documented deviations from conservative orthodoxy found.

0.note: Consistent recorded pro-life votes: FOR 2023 Medicaid-abortion-funding ban amendment; NO on HB195/2026 (pro-life side).

2.note: As Speaker scheduled & her GOP majority passed HB183 trans-girls-in-sports ban (22-18, 2024) — affirms biological sex in sports.

4.note: Led passage of HB183 restricting transgender participation in girls' school sports — opposes LGBTQ-identity policy in schools.

2.note: Introduced HJR7 constitutional spending-cap amendment; pursued dozens of Finance budget cuts toward a constitutional spending limit.

3.note: Opposed eliminating the absentee-ballot witness-signature requirement — favored stricter absentee/mail-ballot integrity.

4.note: Championed HB61 (signed 2023) barring officials from closing gun stores / restricting firearms in declared emergencies — anti-disarmament; NRA-backed.

profile.background: AK Senate District T (rural western/Arctic — Nome/Kotzebue/Utqiagvik) since 2001; physician (MD, Oral Roberts University) and attorney. Co-Chair Senate Finance. Won 2024 unopposed (97.6%); NOT on the 2026 ballot — term runs to Jan 2029. Member of the 2025 bipartisan Senate majority coalition; caucused with Republicans in the majority 2013-14.

donny-olson.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (rural Native Democrat with conservative leanings). SANCTITY: in 2002 testified IN SUPPORT of SB364 (abortion funding restriction) on behalf of Alaska Right to Life, calling abortion 'an inappropriate procedure' as a licensed MD, and voted in committee to advance it -> documented pro-life. PUBLIC JUSTICE: AK Senate passed HB49 (repeal-and-replace of SB91, restoring tougher criminal sentences) 19-0 in May 2019 -> tough-on-crime. FISCAL: broke with Senate Democrats to vote NO on the HB69 $1,000 BSA education increase (Apr 2025) — fiscal restraint, but not a clean school-choice signal -> null. Most non-life/non-crime culture cells: genuine NO EVIDENCE FOUND -> null. Candidacy currency: NOT on 2026 ballot (won 2024, term to Jan 2029) — set candidacy_status incumbent.

0.note: Testified for Alaska Right to Life supporting SB364 (abortion-funding restriction) as an MD calling abortion 'an inappropriate procedure'; voted in committee to advance — documented pro-life conviction.

2.note: AK Senate passed HB49 (repeal/replace SB91, restoring tougher sentences) 19-0 in 2019 — proportional punishment of evildoers.

profile.background: AK Senate District G (Midtown Anchorage) since 2019; former Anchorage Assembly 2008-2017. Chair, Legislative Budget & Audit; bipartisan Senate majority. Self-described '100% pro-choice'. Running for reelection 2026 (one of the 10 staggered seats up).

elvi-gray-jackson.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Consistent urban progressive; documented contrary positions with HARD citations (null where none). SANCTITY: self-described '100% pro-choice'; co-sponsor of SB147/2025-26 (companion to HB195) expanding pharmacist prescribing that pro-life groups call chemical-abortion expansion; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Votes Northwest (2018). MARRIAGE/FAMILY: prime sponsor of SB43 (2023) comprehensive K-12 sex-ed (critics: SOGI/LGBTQ-inclusive standards). PUBLIC JUSTICE: sponsored a package of police use-of-force-restriction bills (chokehold ban SB32/SB1, etc.) in the George Floyd era -> contrary to backing law enforcement. Confirmed NOT a sponsor of the 2023 gun-control bills (HB162/164) — self_defense left null, no inference. No conservative deviations.

0.note: Self-described '100% pro-choice' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Co-sponsor of SB147 (pharmacist prescribing) that pro-life groups characterize as expanding chemical-abortion access — opposite of abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Votes Northwest (2018).

2.note: Prime sponsor of SB43 comprehensive sex-ed including gender-identity content — does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Prime sponsor of SB43 SOGI-inclusive K-12 sex-ed — promotes LGBTQ-identity content in schools.

2.note: Prime sponsor of SB43 comprehensive K-12 sex-ed (National Sexuality Education Standards per critics) — supports, not opposes, comprehensive sex-ed.

0.note: Sponsored a package of police use-of-force-restriction bills (chokehold ban SB32/SB1, shooting-at-vehicles ban, duty-to-intervene) in George Floyd era — contrary to backing law enforcement / opposing curbs.

profile.background: AK Senate District J (East Anchorage) since 2023; former Anchorage Assembly 2016-2023; ran for US House 2014 and Anchorage mayor 2021. Major/JAG, Alaska Army National Guard. Observant Jewish (cites tikkun olam) — Christian Liberty cells N/A to his professed faith, left null. Won 2024; NOT on 2026 ballot, term to Jan 2029. Bipartisan Senate majority.

forrest-dunbar.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION on policing. PUBLIC JUSTICE q0 = TRUE: as Anchorage Assembly member voted to add ~100 sworn APD officers, funded police academies, and explicitly rejected 'defund' (called opponent's defund claim 'embarrassing fabrications') — a Democrat with a documented pro-police-funding record (credit where earned). Contrary (FALSE) cells: SANCTITY — endorsed by Planned Parenthood (2024). MARRIAGE/FAMILY — endorsed by Alaskans Together For Equality, door-knocked to defeat an anti-trans bathroom bill, backed SOGI anti-discrimination + conversion-therapy ban, promotes the Equality Act. SELF-DEFENSE q1 — stated he would support a 'well-crafted Red Flag Law' (with due process) and barring firearms from violent abusers. Christian Liberty pillar null (he is Jewish; no inference). Candidacy currency: NOT on 2026 ballot (won 2024, term to Jan 2029) — corrected candidacy_status from incumbent_seeking_reelection to incumbent.

1.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood (Alliance Advocates, 2024) — pro-abortion-access; opposite of abolition.
Endorsed by Alaskans Together For Equality; called LGBTQ anti-discrimination protections 'absolutely necessary'.

2.note: Door-knocked to defeat an anti-transgender bathroom bill; backed SOGI anti-discrimination and a conversion-therapy ban.

4.note: Promotes the federal Equality Act and SOGI protections — supports LGBTQ-identity policy.

2.note: Actively supported Anchorage SOGI anti-discrimination ordinance — supports SOGI policy.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY: voted to add ~100 APD officers, funded police academies; explicitly rejected defunding the police.

1.note: Stated he would support a 'well-crafted Red Flag Law' with due-process protections and barring firearms from violent abusers — supports red-flag laws.
